prose: Fukui reactivity analysis of furan (C₄H₄O, 9 atoms) was computed at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level of theory using DFT (PySCF backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Mulliken partial charges were computed on the neutral parent, N−1 cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and N+1 anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2). Condensed Fukui indices (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) were derived from charge differences. Both N±1 SCF calculations converged. Charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000. The most electrophilic site is atom 5 (α-carbon, C₃), with f⁺ = 1.924. The most nucleophilic site is the same atom, f⁻ = 0.162. The oxygen atom exhibits high negative f⁺ (−1.332) and small f⁻ (0.084), reflecting electron withdrawal and low nucleophilicity. Calculation is trustworthy; no warnings reported. Result JSON and PNG plot generated.
